summary:
The intentional vs. unintentional habit classification is rooted in behavioral psychology, where intentional habits involve deliberate goals while unintentional ones reflect automated or environment-driven responses. Although no specific originator is cited among the provided names, Finn and Sim’s work aligns most closely with this well-established dual-framework concept used in clinical settings.
